context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements BNPL eligibility filtering (amount ranges and country codes per provider), the Afterpay and Klarna messaging widget components with correct attributes, and the Klarna widget refresh behavior on price changes.

checklist.name: Afterpay min $35 / max $2000
checklist.description: The eligibility logic uses min=35 and max=2000 for Afterpay (not other values)

checklist.name: Klarna min $10 / max $10000
checklist.description: The eligibility logic uses min=10 and max=10000 for Klarna (not other values)

checklist.name: Affirm min $50 / max $17500
checklist.description: The eligibility logic uses min=50 and max=17500 for Affirm (not other values)

checklist.name: Country filter per provider
checklist.description: The eligibility logic filters by both amount AND country code — providers are excluded when the customer's country is not in their supported list

checklist.name: Afterpay placement element
checklist.description: The Afterpay messaging widget uses the <afterpay-placement> custom HTML element (not a div or img)

checklist.name: Afterpay placement attributes
checklist.description: The <afterpay-placement> element includes data-locale, data-currency, and data-amount attributes

checklist.name: Afterpay amount toFixed(2)
checklist.description: The data-amount attribute on <afterpay-placement> uses price.toFixed(2) or equivalent two-decimal formatting

checklist.name: Klarna placement element
checklist.description: The Klarna messaging widget uses the <klarna-placement> custom HTML element

checklist.name: Klarna amount in cents
checklist.description: The data-purchase-amount on <klarna-placement> is in cents (e.g. Math.round(price * 100)), NOT in dollars

checklist.name: Klarna refresh on price change
checklist.description: The Klarna component calls window.Klarna?.OnsiteMessaging?.refresh() (or Klarna.OnsiteMessaging.refresh()) when the price prop/value changes

checklist.name: No widget shown outside range
checklist.description: The messaging components return null (or equivalent) when the price is below the provider's minimum or above the maximum
